message:
fix(freeze): correct JSValue ABI for native 64-bit platforms

The is_wasm32 constant was hardcoded to true, forcing 8-byte JSValue
(NaN-boxing) on all platforms. On native 64-bit (macOS ARM64), QuickJS
uses 16-byte JSValue (struct), causing ABI mismatch and SIGSEGV when
calling frozen functions.

Root cause: Parameter corruption in frozen_dispatch_lookup - var_refs
was shifted by 8 bytes and pointed to garbage.

Also adds end.js to close the polyfill IIFE opened by core.js.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+    // Mark polyfills as initialized
+    globalThis._polyfillsInitialized = true;
+})(); // End of core.js IIFE
